task_completion.description: Verify that Eclipse IDE is open and the 'commons-cli' project is visible in the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Eclipse IDE window with 'commons-cli' listed in the Project Explorer or Package Explorer.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 'build.xml' file has been created in the root of the 'commons-cli' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'build.xml' directly under the 'commons-cli' project node in the Eclipse Project/Package Explorer.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'build.xml' contains the required targets: 'clean', 'compile', and 'jar' with correct paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the editor view of 'build.xml'. It should show XML targets for 'clean' (deleting build dir), 'compile' (compiling to 'build/classes'), and 'jar' (packaging to 'build/commons-cli.jar').

task_completion.description: Verify that the Ant build was executed within Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Eclipse Console view showing Ant build output, typically ending with 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' and showing the execution of the clean, compile, and jar targets.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'commons-cli.jar' file was successfully generated in the 'build' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'build' folder in the Eclipse Project/Package Explorer under 'commons-cli' containing the 'commons-cli.jar' file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Eclipse IDE to create the file and run the build, rather than bypassing it with terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction within the Eclipse GUI (e.g., Eclipse editor tabs, Eclipse Ant view, Eclipse console) rather than a standalone terminal window running 'ant' or 'touch'.

integrity.description: Ensure the Ant build output is genuine and reflects the actual compilation of the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console should show realistic Ant logs, such as 'Compiling 23 source files to .../build/classes' and 'Building jar: .../build/commons-cli.jar', rather than mock or empty output.

integrity.description: Ensure the build.xml was edited inside Eclipse.
integrity.visual_evidence: The build.xml file should be visible in an Eclipse editor tab, not in an external text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is 'commons-cli' located at '/home/ga/eclipse-workspace/commons-cli'. The build.xml file must be in the project root. The resulting JAR must be located at 'build/commons-cli.jar'. A successful build will compile 23 source files into 26 class files, and the resulting JAR file should be approximately 95.2 KB (exceeding the 30 KB minimum requirement). Note: Ignore any metadata suggesting an expected class count of 20; the correct compiled class count is 26.
